items.title: Hundreds of prolific Wikipedia editors are threatening to go on strike
items.summary: Wikipedia is one of the last bastions of trust on the internet. But last week, volunteer editors and contributors were alarmed to hear that a small but important team of engineers at the nonprofit that supports it had been laid off. items.title: This AI startup will clean your home for free to train future robots
items.summary: AI training startup Shift wants to clean your home for free. The catch - because, despite what its website says, there's always a catch - is that it will record cleaners as they scrub, vacuum, dust, tidy, and wash, and use that footage to train robots. items.title: Fonoa raises $110M and buys PwC’s tax software to build a real-time compliance platform
items.summary: The Dublin-registered tax-tech firm paired a Series C led by Headline with the acquisition of PwC’s Indirect Tax Edge, a bet that periodic compliance is giving way to real-time. items.title: This is MSI’s new Claw 8 EX AI Plus gaming handheld
items.summary: MSI has unveiled a new Claw 8 handheld gaming PC ahead of the Computex 2026 show, and this model swaps out its predecessors' Intel Lunar Lake mobile chip for a specialized handheld processor. items.title: Bank of Italy says it is talking to the world’s big AI firms
items.summary: Governor Fabio Panetta used the central bank’s annual assembly to pitch AI as a fix for Italy’s chronic productivity problem, and to say the bank is already engaging the firms building it. items.summary: The Slim 7x (2026) has impressive performance improvements, but its target user - the modern professional - remains the same.

items.title: Adobe’s conversational AI agent is a mediocre design intern
items.summary: AI image tools rarely make me feel like I'm part of the creative process. items.title: Dell rallies on Nvidia-powered AI server demand as it lifts its forecast
items.summary: Dell spent years being valued as a maker of laptops and storage boxes, a solid, unexciting business in a mature market. The AI build-out has rewritten that story in a single quarter. items.title: Norway’s $2.3tn fund backs a human-rights review at Palantir
items.summary: The world’s largest sovereign wealth fund does not often pick a fight with a company it owns. When it does, the size of the holder makes the gesture hard to ignore.
